pattern: Group numbers by their largest decimal digit, then only the two biggest values in each group can ever pair up.
hints: A number's largest digit is easy to compute; think about which numbers are even allowed to pair with which.
Two numbers can only form a candidate pair if their largest digits match, so bucket numbers by that key.
Within a bucket you never need more than the top two values: keep a running best and second-best per key and combine those.

edge_inputs.description: every value shares the same largest digit
several bucket keys, only two viable pairs to compare
minimum length array, no valid pair
three numbers share a key; must pick the top two, not the first two seen
single-digit numbers can still match multi-digit numbers on largest digit
duplicate values in the same bucket

stress.description: 100 elements engineered so most share one largest-digit bucket, forcing the top-two tracking
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, size)
    nums = []
    for _ in range(n):
        d = rng.randrange(1, 10)
        rest_len = rng.randrange(0, 3)
        rest = ''.join(str(rng.randrange(0, d + 1)) for _ in range(rest_len))
        num = int(str(d) + rest) if rest else d
        nums.append(min(num, 10000))
    return [nums]
